tif

Class TagValueFormatter

  • java.lang.Object
    • tif.TagValueFormatter

  • public final class TagValueFormatter
extends java.lang.Object
    Utility methods for converting parsed TIFF metadata values into strongly-typed Java representations.

    Supports conversion of numeric, textual, and rational metadata values commonly found in TIFF, EXIF, DNG, and related image formats.

    Since:
    20 June 2026
    Version:
    1.0
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

      Fields

      Default constructor will always throw an exception.

      static double[] getDoubleArray(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
      Extracts all double-precision decimal values contained within an IFD entry as a double array.

      Returns the first value contained in an IFD entry as a scalar float.

      Returns all values contained in an IFD entry as an integer array.
      static int getIntValue(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
      Returns the first value contained in an IFD entry as an integer.

      Returns all 64-bit numerical values contained within an IFD entry as a long array.

      Returns the first numeric value contained in an IFD entry as a long.

      Extracts all fractional objects contained within an IFD entry as a RationalNumber array.

      Returns the first value contained in an IFD entry as a RationalNumber.

      Parses the entry data into a ZonedDateTime object.

    • Method Detail

      • toByteValue

        public static byte toByteValue(java.lang.Object data)
        Returns the value as a byte.
        Parameters:
        data - the value to convert
        Returns:
        the byte value, or 0 if the value is not a Byte

      • toByteArray

        public static byte[] toByteArray(java.lang.Object data)
        Returns the value as a byte array.
        Parameters:
        data - the value to convert
        Returns:
        the byte array, or an empty array if the value is not a byte[]

      • getIntValue

        public static int getIntValue(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
        Returns the first value contained in an IFD entry as an integer.
        Parameters:
        entry - the entry to evaluate
        Returns:
        the first integer value represented by the entry
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if the entry is null, exceeds the 32-bit integer range, or does not contain a usable integer value

      • getIntArray

        public static int[] getIntArray(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
        Returns all values contained in an IFD entry as an integer array.
        Parameters:
        entry - the entry to evaluate
        Returns:
        the integer values represented by the entry
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if the entry is null, exceeds the 32-bit integer range, or does not contain usable integer values

      • toIntArray

        public static int[] toIntArray(java.lang.Object data)
        Converts a value to an integer array.

        Supports TIFF integer field types that can be represented as 32-bit integers. Unsigned BYTE and SHORT values are widened to preserve their unsigned values.

        Values that require more than 32 bits, such as Long and long[], are not converted.

        Parameters:
        data - the value to convert
        Returns:
        an integer array, an empty array if the value is null or cannot be represented as an integer, or null if the type is unsupported

      • getLongValue

        public static long getLongValue(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
        Returns the first numeric value contained in an IFD entry as a long.
        Parameters:
        entry - the DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD to evaluate
        Returns:
        the first value represented by the entry as a long
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if entry is null, or if the entry does not contain any convertible numeric values

      • getLongArray

        public static long[] getLongArray(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
        Returns all 64-bit numerical values contained within an IFD entry as a long array.
        Parameters:
        entry - the entry to evaluate
        Returns:
        all values represented by the entry as a long array
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if entry is null, or if the entry does not contain any convertible numeric values

      • toLongArray

        public static long[] toLongArray(java.lang.Object data)
        Converts a value to a long array.

        Supports TIFF integer values, numeric types, and rational values that can be represented as long.

        Parameters:
        data - the value to convert
        Returns:
        a long array, an empty array if data is null, or null if the type is unsupported

      • getFloatValue

        public static float getFloatValue(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
        Returns the first value contained in an IFD entry as a scalar float.
        Parameters:
        entry - the entry to evaluate
        Returns:
        the first value represented by the entry as a float
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if entry is null, or if the entry does not contain any convertible numeric values

      • getFloatArray

        public static float[] getFloatArray(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
        Extracts all single-precision decimal values contained within an IFD entry as a float array.
        Parameters:
        entry - the entry to evaluate
        Returns:
        the array of floats containing the entry data elements
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if the entry is null, or contains a value that cannot be converted

      • toFloatArray

        public static float[] toFloatArray(java.lang.Object data)
        Converts a value to a float array.

        Supports floating-point, rational, and other numeric values that can be represented as float.

        Parameters:
        data - the value to convert
        Returns:
        a float array, an empty array if data is null, or null if the type is unsupported

      • getDoubleValue

        public static double getDoubleValue(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
        Returns the first value contained in an IFD entry as a scalar double.
        Parameters:
        entry - the entry to evaluate
        Returns:
        the first double value represented by the entry
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if entry is null, or if the entry does not contain any convertible numeric values

      • getDoubleArray

        public static double[] getDoubleArray(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
        Extracts all double-precision decimal values contained within an IFD entry as a double array.
        Parameters:
        entry - the entry to evaluate
        Returns:
        the array of doubles containing the entry data elements
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if the entry is null, or contains a value that cannot be converted

      • toDoubleArray

        public static double[] toDoubleArray(java.lang.Object data)
        Converts the specified value to an array of doubles.

        Supports floating-point values, rational values, and other numeric types that can be represented as double.

        Parameters:
        data - the value to convert
        Returns:
        a double array, an empty array if data is null, or null if the value type is unsupported

      • getRationalValue

        public static RationalNumber getRationalValue(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
        Returns the first value contained in an IFD entry as a RationalNumber.
        Parameters:
        entry - the entry to evaluate
        Returns:
        the first rational value represented by the entry
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if entry is null, or if the entry does not contain any usable rational values

      • getRationalArray

        public static RationalNumber[] getRationalArray(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
        Extracts all fractional objects contained within an IFD entry as a RationalNumber array.
        Parameters:
        entry - the entry to evaluate
        Returns:
        the rational values represented by the entry
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if the entry is null, or does not contain any usable rational values

      • toRationalArray

        public static RationalNumber[] toRationalArray(java.lang.Object data)
        Converts a value to a rational number array.

        Supports RationalNumber instances and arrays of rational numbers.

        Parameters:
        data - the value to convert
        Returns:
        a rational number array, an empty array if data is null, or null if the type is unsupported

      • toStringValue

        public static java.lang.String toStringValue(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
        Returns an IFD entry's value as a readable text representation.
        Parameters:
        entry - the entry to convert
        Returns:
        a formatted string representation of the entry value

      • toStringValue

        public static java.lang.String toStringValue(java.lang.Object data,
                                             TagHint hint)
        Converts a raw metadata payload value into a trimmed human-readable string representation.
        Parameters:
        data - the raw metadata object value to evaluate
        hint - the structural formatting hint applied to this tag, or null if none
        Returns:
        a formatted text string representation of the data payload

      • getZonedDateTime

        public static java.time.ZonedDateTime getZonedDateTime(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
        Parses the entry data into a ZonedDateTime object. This method supports ISO-8601 and various regional patterns via the SmartDateParser.
        Parameters:
        entry - the entry to parse
        Returns:
        a parsed ZonedDateTime object
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if the entry does not contain a valid date hint, the format is invalid, the entry is null or not convertible to a valid date representation

      • toZonedDateTime

        public static java.time.ZonedDateTime toZonedDateTime(java.lang.Object data)
        Converts a value to a ZonedDateTime.
        Parameters:
        data - the value to convert
        Returns:
        the parsed ZonedDateTime, or null if the data is not a string or cannot be parsed as a date/time

      • decodeUserComment

        public static java.lang.String decodeUserComment(byte[] data)
        Decodes text from an Exif tag, such as UserComment, that begins with an 8-byte character encoding header.

        For UNICODE comments, the Exif specification stores text as UCS-2/UTF-16 using the file's byte order and does not require a Byte Order Mark (BOM). However, some software, including Adobe Photoshop and Windows Explorer, writes a BOM immediately after the encoding header.

        Java does not automatically remove a BOM when decoding with a fixed UTF-16 charset. To avoid a leading '?' phantom character appearing in the decoded text, this method detects and skips BOM markers (0xFEFF or 0xFFFE) and adjusts the character encoding accordingly.

        If no BOM is present, the payload is decoded as UTF-16LE for compatibility with commonly encountered Exif files.

        Parameters:
        data - the raw byte array from the Exif metadata tag
        Returns:
        the decoded and trimmed text string, or an empty string if the input is invalid

      • canConvertToInt

        public static boolean canConvertToInt(TifFieldType type)
        Determines whether values stored using the specified TIFF field type can be represented without loss in a 32-bit signed integer.
        Parameters:
        type - the TIFF field type to evaluate
        Returns:
        true if the field type can be safely converted to an int, otherwise false

      • formatNumericValue

        private static java.lang.String formatNumericValue(double d)
        Formats a numeric value as a readable string.

        Whole numbers are displayed without a decimal point. Decimal values are rounded to four decimal places, with trailing zeros removed. Special values such as NaN and infinity are returned unchanged.

        Parameters:
        d - the value to format
        Returns:
        the formatted string representation

      • decodeIntArray

        private static java.lang.String decodeIntArray(java.lang.Object val)
        Converts an integer value or integer array into a space-separated string.
        Parameters:
        val - the value to convert
        Returns:
        a space-separated string of integer values, or an empty string if no values are available
